unit.name: Function approximation, gradients, Jacobians, and chain rule
unit.description: Explains why nonlinearities are required and builds the multivariable-calculus toolkit needed for gradient-based learning.
unit.summary: This segment first argues that neural networks are function approximators and that nonlinearities are essential because a stack of pure matrix multiplies collapses to a single linear transform, giving no extra representational power [ts=903s][ts=939s][ts=952s]. The lecture then introduces stochastic gradient descent as the core learning update and asks how to compute gradients for arbitrary composed functions [ts=1028s][ts=1046s]. To make that possible, multivariable calculus is reframed as single-variable calculus carried out with vectors and matrices [ts=1228s]. The instructor reviews scalar derivatives, then defines gradients as vectors of partial derivatives for scalar-output functions and Jacobians as matrices of partial derivatives for multi-output functions [ts=1388s][ts=1400s][ts=1463s]. The section ends by showing that composition is handled by the chain rule, with Jacobians multiplying just as ordinary derivatives do in the scalar case [ts=1536s][ts=1615s].

key_points.text: Without nonlinearities, composing multiple matrix multiplies is equivalent to a single linear transform, so depth adds no representational power.
Learning is presented through the stochastic gradient descent update using the gradient of the objective function.
For a scalar-output function with n inputs, the gradient is the vector of partial derivatives with respect to each input.
For a function with n inputs and m outputs, the Jacobian is the matrix of output-by-input partial derivatives.
For composed multivariable functions, the chain rule multiplies the relevant Jacobians.

The lecture then introduces stochastic gradient descent as the core learning update and asks how to compute gradients for arbitrary composed functions [ts=1028s][ts=1046s]. To make that possible, multivariable calculus is reframed as single-variable calculus carried out with vectors and matrices [ts=1228s]. The instructor reviews scalar derivatives, then defines gradients as vectors of partial derivatives for scalar-output functions and Jacobians as matrices of partial derivatives for multi-output functions [ts=1388s][ts=1400s][ts=1463s]. The section ends by showing that composition is handled by the chain rule, with Jacobians multiplying just as ordinary derivatives do in the scalar case [ts=1536s][ts=1615s].

kp_catalog.name: Activation functions and nonlinearities
kp_catalog.description: Differentiable nonlinear activation functions such as sigmoid, tanh, ReLU, and ReLU-like variants are needed so networks can model nonlinear functions and support gradient-based learning. The idea that activations between linear maps prevent the network from collapsing into a single linear transformation and allow nonlinear decision boundaries.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because activation functions and nonlinearities formalizes how differentiable nonlinear activation functions such as sigmoid, tanh, ReLU, and ReLU-like variants are needed so networks can model nonlinear functions and support gradient-based learning, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.

kp_catalog.name: Gradient descent and stochastic gradient descent
kp_catalog.description: Neural networks are trained by computing the gradient of an objective and updating parameters with stochastic gradient descent. Gradient-based optimization updates parameters by subtracting a scaled gradient, with the learning rate controlling step size.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because gradient descent and stochastic gradient descent formalizes how neural networks are trained by computing the gradient of an objective and updating parameters with stochastic gradient descent, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Gradient and Jacobian in vectorized calculus
kp_catalog.description: For multivariable functions, scalar outputs use gradient vectors and multi-output functions use Jacobian matrices of partial derivatives.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because gradient and Jacobian in vectorized calculus formalizes how for multivariable functions, scalar outputs use gradient vectors and multi-output functions use Jacobian matrices of partial derivatives, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Chain rule for composed functions
kp_catalog.description: Gradients of composed scalar or vector functions are computed by multiplying derivatives or Jacobians along the computation path.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because chain rule for composed functions formalizes how gradients of composed scalar or vector functions are computed by multiplying derivatives or Jacobians along the computation path, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
